topPosts.text: RISEx is the fastest onchain perps exchange I've used. Sub-millisecond execution is real.

現在、招待制のアーリーPerp DEX、 @risextrade に参加しています。
招待コードを5つ持っているので、触ってみたい方はDMください😊

RISExは、RISE Chain上のオンチェーンOrderbook型Perps DEX。

個人的に気になっているのは、
・招待制のPrivate Beta
・取引量 / OI / ROIベースのleaderboard
・Founding 25のOG Trader選抜（現在開催中）
・RISExとRISE Chainへの早期関与
・Perps取引の利用実績
このあたり。

資金調達面も強く、RISE ChainはFinality Capital leadのSeed、Galaxy参加のStrategic roundなどを経て、累計$8M以上を調達。
支援者にはGalaxy、Finality Capital、P2 Ventures / Polygon Venturesのほか、Ethereum共同創設者のVitalik Buterin氏、Polygon共同創設者のSandeep Nailwal氏、Aave創業者のStani Kulechov氏などの名前も出ています。

また、Hyperliquidのような独自実行環境とは違い、EVMの中でorder / matching / settlementを実行しながら、DeFiとの連携も狙っている点が面白いです。

まだアーリーで板が薄いため、いきなり大きく触るというより、板・スプレッドを見ながら小さく触るのが良さそうです👀

RISExは、EVMオンチェーンPerps市場への先回り案件として見ています👀
＃エアドロ #アーリー #PerpDEX

🧵 Article Summary 

1. @risextrade is the first killer app built on a base layer called MarketCore. The real product isn't the exchange (RISEx), it's the mold that stamps out exchanges (MarketCore), the same way Uniswap sat on the AMM and apps sit on the App Store.

2. Hyperliquid's HIP-3 proved the massive demand for permissionless market creation (May 2026 OI of $2.47B, with @tradexyz holding 94%), but because HyperCore and the EVM keep separate state, the markets it creates are trapped behind glass and can't compose.

3. MarketCore does the same permissionless creation, but markets are born inside a single EVM-native state, becoming Lego bricks instantly composable with Morpho, Yearn, and Curve in the same block and same transaction.

4. On top of that you get RWA collateral loops, pre-IPO perps as collateral, self-driving index funds, reflexive markets, structured products, and digital commodities like compute and electricity. Market = vocabulary, composability = grammar, strategy = sentence, so the combinations aren't N+M but N×M, diverging toward infinity.

5. A shared orderbook solves liquidity fragmentation (islands into a continent) and spins the flywheel, until the only bottleneck left isn't the exchange, the chain, or blockspace, but human imagination asking "what can't be a market?"
@risextrade composability matters. always.
@risextrade Compatibility always matters. In fact, it matters most.

A month ago in my tg (link in bio), I shared my honest opinion on why I won’t be farming @risextrade 

I have nothing against the tech, but from a farming perspective, I believe there are projects with better +EV

1. RiseX has been hyped from the beginning and has seen pretty decent volume since Day 1, so the hedge basically disappears (it reminds me of the Pacifica hype back then)

2. RiseX won’t have its own token. The main token will be RISE (@risechain, the L2 token). That’s why I don’t think the genesis drop will be as large as those of other Perp DEXs. They have an entire ecosystem to reward, with several dApps set to join the RISE ecosystem soon

3. They did an IDO on Kaito at a $200M valuation. Even if the intentions were good, we’ve seen many examples in the past of these public rounds turning into EL. That’s why I don’t expect a big valuation at launch either

4. The only Perp DEX + L2 token model I’m following right now is INK, which I believe has the best EV and I’m staying committed to that thesis

I opened a ticket at @risextrade Discord about it and they checked the situation out. They just refunded me for my loss. Their support is incredible and I am so grateful. 
Mistakes can always happen, but what truly matters is how you handle them and find a solution. 🙏 https://t.co/LdiHFi1L6b

The @risechain @risextrade - Most people describe Shreds as a faster confirmation mechanism. 

I think that's underselling what's actually happening.

Shreds transform blockchain execution from a batch system into a streaming system.  

1. Traditional blockchains operate like batch processors.

🔹Transactions wait.
🔹A block forms.
🔹The block finishes.
🔹Then the network responds. 

State updates arrive in bursts.  

2. Shreds change the timing model. 

Instead of waiting for a completed block, execution state is propagated continuously as work is performed.

Confirmation becomes a stream not an event.  

3. This matters because latency is not just a UX problem. It's a coordination problem.

Market makers and liquidators and arbitrage systems and traders all depend on how quickly shared state updates propagate through the network.  

4. The interesting part is not simply that confirmations arrive in ~1ms. 

It's that economically secured state information becomes available before block completion.

That reduces the gap between execution and awareness.  

5. Viewed this way Shreds are not merely making blocks faster. 

They are reducing the importance of the block as the primary unit of interaction.

The network starts behaving less like a sequence of batches and more like a continuous real time system.

That's a deeper architectural shift than speed alone.

RiseX might be one of the few Perp-Dex where you still genuinely early.

Most no-token perp projects get overcrowded before the majority even notices them. RiseX hasn't launched its points program yet - and that where the opportunity lies.

Built on RiseChain, @risextrade is a decentralized spot + perpetual exchange backed by Vitalik Buterin, Galaxy, MH Ventures, the CEO of Aave, and others, with $9.2M raised.

Current metrics:

• $1B+ cumulative volume
• $40–50M daily volume
• $5.5M TVL
• $7M OI
• 0.01% maker / 0.03% taker fees
• Invite-only access

If RiseX decides to reward pre-points activity retroactively, early users could be in a much stronger position than those joining once the campaign goes live.
With the points program expected in the next 2–3 weeks, this may be one of the best windows to test the platform before competition ramps up.
